_comment: Candidate client-side tool definition for ACS-generic-neural (13809d4b-6b6d-4297-b95c-a934bceef0b4). Schema copied verbatim from https://www.algolia.com/doc/guides/algolia-ai/agent-studio/how-to/tools/client-side-tools (get_user_cart/add_to_cart examples), field names unchanged (OpenAI function-calling spec). Task 3 will attempt to add this tool to the agent config and drive a /completions round-trip to see whether the agent actually pauses and returns a tool-call for the caller to answer.

function.name: get_technical_handoff_context
function.description: Retrieves additional client-side context (e.g. which code panel/tab the user currently has open) before routing a deep technical question to ACS-technical-neural. Client (browser) is expected to answer this call, not the server.

_resume_pattern_source: https://www.algolia.com/doc/guides/algolia-ai/agent-studio/how-to/tools/mcp-tools (documents the wire-level pause/resume pattern for MCP tool approvals; client-side 'function' tools are documented to use the same conceptual round-trip via the InstantSearch addToolResult() helper, but the docs do not spell out the raw HTTP body for that specific case). There is NO separate endpoint -- the caller re-POSTs to the SAME /completions endpoint with the full message history plus the new tool result/approval field.

_example_resume_request_shape_from_docs._note: This exact shape is documented for MCP tool requiresApproval flows. Task 3 must verify empirically whether client-side function tools use an analogous 'toolApprovals'-like field, or a different field (e.g. a tool result content part), when re-POSTed.
